pages.title: About Me
pages.content:
Meet K.M. Quinn

K.M. Quinn lives on the east coast where she was born and raised. She is a devoted wife, mother, and grandmother. K.M. began writing her first novel Torn Robes: Choices in 2004 between work and raising a family. The second novel in the series Torn Robes: Reckoning will be released in the Fall of 2023.
Along with writing her next project, K.M. enjoys spending time with her family, horseback riding, kayaking, walking, snowshoeing, and being at the beach. Having grown up along the coast the ocean has been her constant companion and still to this day her, “happy place.” She is an avid researcher and loves history along with learning and exploring new things.
K.M. firmly believes that with God everything is possible she has witnessed it firsthand in her own life. Moving from one career to the next God has been preparing K.M. for such a time as this. She finds it an absolute joy, pleasure, and honor to share God’s undying love with her readers.

Embracing the legendary Sword in the Stone. Regardless of where we are or where we go, it's all about choices!

pages.title: Books
pages.content:
TORN ROBES: CHOICES

Torn Robes: Choices

Gilford high school students are in peril!

The township of Gilford is demanding action! Principal Krystina Davis puts her career on the line and convinces the board to hire best-selling author of Children of the King, Nebraska Pastor John Wheaton.

Pastor Wheaton has one semester to put his God-centered approach to guiding teens to the test.

Initially, he receives a 'warm' New England welcome. Then the real work begins. Can Pastor John make a difference for the troubled teens of Gilford? If not, what will happen to the students and families who so desperately need him?

Buy Now
Signed Copy

Torn Robes: Choices Companion Guide

The Companion Guide is designed to help people discuss challenging topics and reflect on personal journeys. The questions are open-ended, without right or wrong answers. What's important is how you respond to them.

Whether you're using this guide for Christian camps, youth groups, counseling, or seeking guidance on navigating peer pressure and social media, my aim is to offer insightful perspectives that encourage deep thinking and reflection.

Above all, I hope to help you realize and appreciate how special and loved you really are.

Buy Now

Reviews

Highly recommend this inspirational, thought-provoking book dealing with the issues youth face today, and showing how the truth of God's Word can transform any situation. Truly a remarkable read! This book is part of a series, and I am anxious to read the next book in the series!

— MM, Amazon Reader

Well written story highlighting the miriad of issues that young adults face and choices they must make. It is the individual characters that must make their own choices, no ones doctrine is forced on them. The characters feel very real. Looking forward to the next installment.

—Joyce B., Amazon Reader

Easy reading. i enjoyed the storyline and the reality of the chatacters. I was very impressed with the unique way the pastor in the story brought the youth group together through the Bible. Iwant to bring this book to our pastor and youth group. Looking forward to the next book.

— Claudia J., amazon Reader

TORN ROBES: RECKONING

Torn Robes: Reckoning

Pride goes before destruction, a haughty spirit before a fall.

Proverbs 16:18

The winds are blowing toward the east coast, where a storm is indeed brewing. Dark, ominous clouds are drifting inland from the Atlantic Ocean, a foreshadowing, perhaps? Unaware of the events about to descend upon their unassuming town. The residents of Gilford are preparing for the holiday season and the much-anticipated championship game. Some are planning meals and arranging family gatherings, while others are dealing with their own stubbornness and anger. And still, there are some, like the Tin Man, who have no heart and are plotting and scheming for the ruin and downfall of others. Prayers have gone up to Heaven, petitioning Almighty God for a miracle. The people of Gilford are at a crossroads, and the time has come to make a choice. A storm is coming…Gilford, are you ready for the Reckoning?

Buy Now
Signed Copy

Torn Robes: Reckoning Companion Guide

The Companion Guide is designed to help people discuss challenging topics and reflect on personal journeys. The questions are open-ended, without right or wrong answers. What's important is how you respond to them.

Whether you are utilizing this guide for Christian camps, youth groups, counseling, or seeking personal guidance on navigating peer pressure and social media, my aim is to offer insightful perspectives that encourage deep thinking and reflection.

Above all, I hope to help you realize and appreciate how special and loved you are.

Buy Now

Untold: Bible Stories Reimagined

Untold: Bible Stories Reimagined: A Time For Everything

"A Time For Everything," my contribution to the anthology "Untold Bible Stories Reimagined," is a journey of kingship and redemption.

In this reimagined tale, a young Solomon uncovers the heartbreaking truth behind the brief life of his older brother. His parents, King David and Queen Bathsheba, struggle with their own painful past as they turn to prayer, seeking God's guidance.

“A Time For Everything” explores the complexities of family, loss, and the quest for divine wisdom.

Buy Now

Reviews

Entertaining book of short stories told from the perspective of other characters in the original Bible story. Thought provoking, as I had never considered the background stories of others in the story. Stories are detailed, but short enough to easily complete. Each writer's style fits their story and the writing flows well, helping you to imagine the story as it unfolds. A recommended read!

— Sandra B., Amazon Reader

This was an enjoyable read. Each story added details that brought the story to life. The basic story remained true to the Bible yet filled it out with creative details. The unique viewpoint of each story was refreshing. I would recommend this to anyone looking for a novel perspective.

—CJSW, Amazon Reader

I thoroughly enjoyed these Bible stories told in a reimagined way. The stories are told in the style of historical fiction, each writer in this anthology adding his or her creativity to the story without changing any biblical truths. These writings are a fresh way to read and internalize these beloved Bible stories.

— Nancy K., Amazon Reader

pages.content:
Vermont Memories:

An Exciting Weekend Filled With Books & Art!

I want to express my deepest gratitude to the wonderful people of Vermont who graciously hosted me, along with fellow author Kelly Mitchell Schilling, who wrote “Paulie ’s Tale Tail,” the first book in the Tales of Paulie series, and the incredibly talented illustrator and artist John Collado. Our whirlwind weekend of book signings was truly unforgettable. Your support and enthusiasm made this experience extraordinary!

Author's At the Vineyard!

It was a wonderful day meeting fellow authors and everyone who braved the snow to attend the 2023 Author's at the Vineyard event.

pages.title: Jane Austin
pages.content:
I'm currently rereading some of my favorite novels. I am a huge fan of Jane Austen; I can picture myself strolling through the picturesque fields of the English countryside—book in hand, mesmerized by the words popping off the page. I can relate to the heroines in her stories as I understand the feeling of not expecting much but still harboring dreams of the seemingly impossible. I recently finished "Lady Susan," which may not be one of her most renowned novels; however, I found it brilliant in its delivery. The book is composed of letters exchanged between the characters, and although I was initially unsure about this format, I found it incredibly intimate and revealing. After all, don't we reveal ourselves more personally through a letter? I found myself eagerly anticipating each new letter as the storyline unfolded.

Just wanted to share in case there are any other Jane Austen enthusiasts out there!

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.content:
K.M. Quinn

PRIVACY POLICY

At K.M. Quinn, protecting your privacy is very important. This privacy statement applies to our collection, use, and sharing of personal information we receive from visitors and users of this website.

This privacy policy has been compiled to better serve those who are concerned with how their ‘Personally Identifiable Information' (PII) is being used online.  PII, as described in US privacy law and information security, is information that can be used on its own or with other information to identify, contact, or locate a single person, or to identify an individual in context. Please read our privacy policy carefully to get a clear understanding of how we collect, use, protect or otherwise handle your Personally Identifiable Information in accordance with our website.

If you disagree with any aspect of this privacy policy, please discontinue use of this website.

What personal information do we collect from the people that visit our website?

When visiting our site, as appropriate, you may be asked to enter your name, email address or other details to help you with your user experience.

When do we collect information?

We collect information from you when you register on our site, subscribe to a newsletter or email list, respond to a survey, fill out a form or otherwise enter information on our site.

When you provide us with feedback on our products or services.

How do we use your information?

We may use the information we collect from you when  you register, make a purchase, sign up for our newsletter, respond to a survey or marketing communication, surf the website, or use certain other site features in the following ways:

To improve our website in order to better serve you.

To quickly process your transactions.

To send periodic emails regarding orders, new blog posts, and other marketing communications.

Except as otherwise stated in the privacy policy, we do not sell, trade, rent or otherwise share your Personal Information with third parties without your consent.

How do we protect your information?

We only provide articles and information. We never ask for credit card numbers.

We use regular Malware Scanning from our web hosting service.

Your personal information is contained behind secured networks and is only accessible by a limited number of persons who have special access rights to such systems, and are required to keep the information confidential. In addition, all sensitive/credit information you supply is encrypted via Secured Socket Layer (SSL) technology.

We implement a variety of security measures when a user enters, submits, or accesses their information to maintain the safety of your personal information.

All transactions are processed through a gateway provider and are not stored or processed on our servers.

Do we use ‘cookies'?

Yes. Cookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer's hard drive through your web browser (if you allow) that enables the site's or service provider's systems to recognize your browser and capture and remember certain information. For example, we use cookies to help us compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interactions so that we can offer better site experiences and tools in the future.

We use cookies to compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interactions. We may also use trusted third-party services to track this information on our behalf. You can choose to have your computer warn you each time a cookie is being sent or can choose to turn off all cookies. You can do this through your browser settings. Each browser is set up a little differently so please read your browser's Help Menu to learn the correct way to modify your cookies.

If you turn cookies off it may turn off some of the features of this site.

Third-Party Disclosure

We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your Personally Identifiable Information unless we provide users with advance notice. This does not include web hosting partners and other parties who assist us in operating our website, conducting our business, or serving our users, so long as those parties agree to keep this information confidential. We may also release information when its release is appropriate to comply with the law, enforce our site policies, or protect ours or others' rights, property or safety. However, non-personally identifiable visitor information may be provided to other parties for marketing, advertising, or other uses.

Social Sharing – Social Sharing buttons are provided by plugins operated and managed by a third-party. If you click on one of the social sharing buttons, certain PII may be stored by the service operating that plugin in accordance with their terms of service and use.

Third-Party Links

This website does include and/or offer third-party products or services. These third-party sites have separate and independent privacy policies. We, therefore, have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites. Nonetheless, we seek to protect the integrity of our site and welcome any feedback about these sites.

Google Analytics

We, along with third-party vendors such as Google use first-party cookies (such as Google Analytics) and third-party cookies or other third-party identifiers together to compile data regarding user interactions with ad impressions and other ad service functions as they relate to our website.

Opting Out: Users can set preferences for how Google advertises to your using the Google Ad Settings page. Alternatively, you can opt out by visiting the Network Advertising Initiative Opt-Out page or by using the Google Analytics Opt-Out Browser Add-On.

California Online Privacy Protection Act

CalOPPA is the first state law in the nation to require commercial websites and online services to post a privacy policy. The law's reach stretches well beyond California to require any person or company in the United States (and conceivably the world) that operates websites collecting PII from California consumers to post a conspicuous privacy policy on its website stating exactly the information being collected and those individuals or companies with whom it is being shared – Read more here.

According to CalOPPA, we agree to the following:

Users can visit our site anonymously.

Once this privacy policy is created, we will add a link to it on our homepage or, at a minimum, on the first significant page after entering our website.

Our privacy policy link includes the word “Privacy” and can easily be found on the page specified above.

You will be notified of any Privacy Policy changes:

On our Privacy Policy page

Via email, if you are subscribed to our email list

You can change your personal information:

By emailing us

By logging into your account and making the changes

COPPA (Children Online Privacy Protection Act)

When it comes to the collection of personal information from children under the age of 13 years old. the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) puts parents in control. The Federal Trade Commission, United States' consumer protection agency, enforces the COPPA Rule, which spells out what operators of websites and online services must do to protect children's privacy and safety online.

We do not specifically market to children under the age of 13 years old.

Fair Information Practices

The Fair Information Practices Principles form the backbone of privacy law in the United States and the concepts they include have played a significant role in the development of data protection laws around the globe. Understanding the Fair Information Practice Principles and how they should be implemented is critical to comply with various privacy laws that protect personal information.

In order to be in line with Fair Information Practices we will take the following responsive action, should a data breach occur:

We will notify you via on-site notification within one business day and via email within 7 business days

We also agree to the Individual Redress Principle which requires that individuals have the right to legally pursue enforceable rights against data collectors and processors who fail to adhere to the law. This principle requires not only that individuals have enforceable rights against data users, but also that individuals have recourse to courts or government agencies to investigate and/or prosecute non-compliance by data processors.

CAN-SPAM Act

The CAN-SPAM Act is a law that sets rules for commercial email, establishes requirements for commercial messages, gives recipients the right to have emails stopped by being sent to them, and spells out tough penalties for violations.

We collect your email address in order to:

Send information, respond to inquiries, and/or other requests or questions.

Process orders and to send information and updates pertaining to orders.

Send you additional information related to your product/service.

Market to our mailing list or continue to send emails to our clients after the original transaction has occurred.

To be in accordance with CAN-SPAM, we agree to the following:

Not use false or misleading subjects or email addresses.

Identify the message as an advertisement in some reasonable way.

Include the physical address of our business or site headquarters.

Monitor third-party email marketing services for compliance if one is used.

Honor opt-out/unsubscribe requests quickly.

Allow users to unsubscribe easily via a link at the bottom of each email.

If at any time you would like to unsubscribe from receiving future emails, you can also email us at info@kmquinn44.com

Contacting Us

If there are any questions regarding this privacy policy, you may contact us via the address below:

K.M. Quinn

PO Box 123

Wherever, NH 12345

info@kmquinn44.com

pages.title: The Thought Hub
pages.content:
Feel The Spirit!

Life is full of choices. Each choice a journey. Every journey a lesson learned.

Share Your Mind

In the midst of today's chaotic world, I find solace in the simple things. During the summer, my husband dug a heart shape for me on our front lawn. You'll notice an iris in full bloom in the middle of the heart, even though it's well past the blooming season. This beautiful inconsistency serves as a reminder to me of God's power and grace, especially during difficult times.

Jane Austin

I'm currently rereading some of my favorite novels. I am a huge fan of Jane Austen; I can picture myself strolling through the picturesque fields of the English countryside—book in hand, mesmerized by the words popping off the page…

Read More

Devotable by K.S. Wuertz

We all believe we are independent. We face the challenging circumstances of living and making choices every day. When will I get up? What is for breakfast? From morning’s rising to evening’s retiring: we choose. God gifted us with the free will to do so and the autonomy to see it all through. Yet, Paul says he is convinced that nothing can separate us from the love of God and Christ Jesus. So no external, or internal forces can separate us from God’s love.

Read More

Truth? What is Truth?

Jesus said to Pilate, "You are right in saying I am a king. In fact, for this reason, I was born and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone on the side of truth listens to me."
"What is truth?" Pilate asked. (John 18:37:38)

pages.title: Truth? What is Truth?
pages.content:
Jesus said to Pilate, "You are right in saying I am a king. In fact, for this reason, I was born and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone on the side of truth listens to me."

"What is truth?" Pilate asked. (John 18:37:38)

In today's age, we live in a world where information is readily available at the click of a button. The velocity at which news spreads seems to be escalating with each passing day. It is crucial that we exercise caution in distinguishing between fact and fiction. Is it possible to maintain an open mind, question our own judgments, and engage in constructive conversations?

These are my truths: The Lord of the Heavens created me. I am of royal birth as a child of the Most High King. Jesus, God's son, is the way, the truth, and the life.

With the guidance and strength of Jesus' love, I can achieve anything that comes my way. His unwavering support and infinite wisdom serve as my constant source of inspiration, giving me the confidence to tackle any challenge that lies ahead. With Jesus by my side, I know I can overcome obstacles and achieve great things. His love guides me on my journey and fills my heart with joy and peace.

What is your Truth?

posts.title: The Blogging Bug
posts.content:
It's finally happened. I've caught the blogging bug and decided to launch my own blog. But before I embark on this journey, I want to explain my reasons for starting it.

You might wonder why I would choose to create a blog while I'm in the midst of writing the third book in the Torn Robes series, titled "The King’s Highway."

Why indeed?

Full disclosure…as an author, I love immersing myself in the fictional world I’ve created, but aside from my family, no one gets to see this side of me—my passion and excitement as I become captivated by the characters and the directions they take me. Why did I choose the Northeast coast as the setting for the many characters who bring the Torn Robes saga to life? And while we’re at it, why did I choose the title Torn Robes?

These questions and many more will be answered on my blog. Each post will provide new insights into the characters: what makes them who they are and why the special town of Gilford matters.

Stay tuned for my upcoming post titled "Why, Torn Robes?" as we dive into the book series "Torn Robes: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town."

I can't wait!

posts.title: Torn Robes: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.
posts.content:
Why, Torn Robes?

Many characters in “Torn Robes: Choices and Reckoning” experience pain, deep emotional turmoil, and grief. The custom of tearing one’s robes signifies profound grief, mourning, or shame. As a Christian author, I wanted to honor God through my work. After all, it was the passion He placed inside me that ignited the creation of “Torn Robes: Choices.”

One of the most tragic events in the Bible, is the story of King David’s daughter, Tamar. She was a princess who faced unbearable shame and grief, circumstances beyond her control. After being abused by her brother, she left his home in a state of devastation; weeping, tearing her decorative garment, and putting ashes on her head. How desolate she must have felt, betrayed by someone she loved and trusted.

This Bible story is one of many that illustrate how tragedy does not discriminate, regardless of who you are or your station in life. Tamar's life experience resonates with our experiences today, doesn't it? Although time moves forward, feelings of pain and betrayal remain timeless. The tearing of one's robe expresses that torment and grief.  

In the Torn Robes books, Choices and Reckoning. I wanted to convey that we have the power to decide whether our grief or emotional turmoil will prevail. I emphasize that the choices we make define who we are. By expressing our feelings, as people did in ancient times by tearing their robes, we can release that pain rather than letting it control us, keeping us bitter and angry.

Am I suggesting that everyone should go around tearing their clothes? No! However, what I am saying is that pain and hurt are too destructive to keep bottled up inside. Even in ancient times, people understood the importance of releasing pain and leaving it at the altar of God. Will this take care of everything? No, but it is a good starting point.

Next time...we'll be visiting the town of Gilford.

posts.title: Torn Robes: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.
posts.content:
The Town of Gilford

One of my first decisions in writing Torn Robes: Choices was deciding where the story would take place. Before I knew it, the colorful town of Gilford was born.

A fictional town, Gilford, is located along the northeast coastline. Where rocky cliffs meet sandy beaches and shimmering blue waters. This picturesque scene can feel inviting one moment and turn into a dark, ferocious force the next.

I understand this, all too well. "The sea is a fickle mistress," as the renowned author Scott O'Dell penned. I grew up along the northeast coastline and find it impossible to be far from it for too long. The ocean, one of God's most precious creations, brings me peace when I am searching for answers or strolling along the water’s edge feeling the sand beneath my feet. However, my youngest daughter can’t stand the feeling of sand on her feet, just like my grandmother. Isn’t it funny how traits get passed down?

I find joy in the smell of its salty air, and yes, it has a distinctive smell, and when the breeze blows, you can taste the salt upon your lips. 

As a child and young adult, I spent countless days at the beach with friends and family; the treasured memories linger to this day. I recall watching the fireworks lit from a huge barge in the water while we sat on a blanket spread out in the soft sand. The brilliant colors would explode across the night sky, making the stars seem small in comparison.

It's truly my happy place!

As with any city or town, Gilford needed a past. The northeast coastline is dotted with small fishing towns of old, rich in tradition, stories, and experiences filled with struggles and success.

That is exactly the history I wanted to create for Gilford. The following is an excerpt from Torn Robes: Choices, as two main characters travel through Gilford for the first time.

Krystina took a right-hand turn onto a busy, but scenic street. People bustled in and out of shops and restaurants. Some people strolled in a slow casual stride and others stopped to glance inside the decorated store fronts displaying the shop’s wares. A few motels and inns were sprinkled about.

Krystina did her best to give her guests a guided tour. They drove along through the different sights and sounds that made up Gilford.

Taking it all in, John remembered some of the history he and Sarah had read regarding the town. At the stop sign at the end of the street, the sea lay out before them. Its vast beauty extended beyond the horizon. Krystina turned left. Spotting a parking space beside the sidewalk along the oceanfront, she pulled in. Her guests had a glorious view. "Beautiful, isn't it," Krystina asked. "The ocean has many moods and should always be respected. She is glorious and wondrous to behold. As recipients and trustees of this portion of the Atlantic, Gilford has reaped her bountiful rewards and been the student of countless lessons." Krystina pointed to the right. The harbor was littered with fishing boats coming and going. Fishermen were unloading the day's catch at the warehouses. The trio got out of the car to stretch their legs. Sarah and John watched the vibrant activity play out.

"It looks like the fishing business is still very much alive."

"Not like it once was. Still, it's a major part of Gilford's history and a generational way of life. The lighthouse over there on the jetty," Krystina pointed out, "is a welcome sign for tired fishermen after a long exhausting trip. If you look off into the distance, there's a cruise ship ready to descend upon the harbor." Krystina motioned, holding her hand over her eyes to shield them from the blazing sun.

I wanted Gilford to be one of the once-thriving fishing towns renowned for their bustling harbors and colorful fishing vessels. Where wives and mothers of fishermen stood on the widow's peak atop their homes, anxiously looking for signs of their loved ones' return. Life at sea is challenging and has taken a heavy toll on Gilford. Many families have lost loved ones to the sea, resulting in broken homes and poverty.

As they struggle to adapt to the new ways of life, the people of the town, young and old, have been forgotten by their town officials.

At first glance, it may seem like everything is going well for this small New England town, but all is not as it appears. Like a beautiful painting, the truth that lies beneath the colors and shapes is rarely what you might imagine it to be.

Gilford is a community at a crossroads, facing challenges that leave its youth searching for answers in all the wrong places. Next time, we'll meet one of the individuals who makes up a piece of the fabric, that is Gilford, revealing the heart and soul of this struggling town.

posts.title: Torn Robes: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.
posts.content:
A Woman of Noble Character: Meet Louise Taylor

If you've read my book, Torn Robes: Choices, you may expect that the first character I will discuss is Pastor John, the protagonist of the story. However, Pastor John would not have made his journey to Gilford without the influence of the strong-willed woman of faith, Louise Taylor.

Louise Taylor is the wife of Dwayne, her high school sweetheart, and the mother of two children: Dwayne Jr., known as D.J., who is a second-year college student at Worcester Polytechnic Institute, and Sharise, a high school student. Louise is employed as a school nurse at Gilford High School. While she can be tough on those she cares for, they always know where she stands and that she will fight for them with her last breath. Although small in stature, she possesses a large spirit. Those who know her respect her and have a healthy fear of her fierce nature when defending those she loves.

In the first book, Torn Robes: Choices, we are introduced to Louise Taylor. She is attending a meeting with the Superintendent and school board members to discuss potential solutions for the troubled teens in Gilford. Louise is described as the Superintendent's "constant irritant," as demonstrated in the following excerpt where she speaks about the teens at Gilford High School.

Spencer sighed. “Haaaahh! It's obvious Louise was adding a bit of comic relief to the evening. But if there is something a little more realistic, you’d like to add Louise, by all means.” Spencer motioned his right hand gesturing the floor was all hers.

“Oh, I’d like to add something alright.” Louise stood up, all five feet, four inches tall. “These are children we’re talking about. We need to get to the root of the problem, not glaze over it with a Band-Aid or look the other way and hope it gets better. This solution is as bad as their behavior. You’re giving them free rein by telling them they’re in charge. These kids show no respect for themselves or the adults in their lives.” Louise motioned her hand like she was cutting off a head. “Well, it’s not okay with me! There’s nothing but trouble ahead.” She wanted to laugh at some of the shocked faces staring back at her. Didn’t they know her by now?

In my third book, “Torn Robes: The King's Highway,” the following excerpt from Chapter Two highlights Louise's concern for her daughter, Sharise. Since her friend Sarah has returned home to Nebraska, Sharise has been missing her. Naturally, Louise's busybody side emerges as she tries to help Sharise through this difficult time.

Louise sat down next to her daughter on the swing that hung from the rafters above. ”Hm, hm.”

“Mom, really? Here you go again, poking where you shouldn’t. Isn’t there a Bible verse for that?

After a long conversation, Louise helps Sharise figure things out, and Sharise is thankful. However, Louise did not forget what her daughter said to her at the beginning of their discussion.

“Oh, mom! Thank you!” Sharise screeched embracing her mother in an enthusiastic hug.

“And Sharise?”

“Yes?”

“There is a scripture for pokey busy bodies…

 We hear that some of you are living in idleness. You are not busy working —you are busy interfering in other people’s lives! 3. 2 Thessalonians 3:11-13

“In case you run across one…“Louise smiled wittingly.”

While she is not perfect—being spicy when necessary and sometimes stubborn to a fault—her heart is always in the right place. Although she may interfere when she shouldn't, her intentions are always good.

Louise reminds me a lot of my grandmother—a resilient, devoted woman who dedicated her life to her family and worked tirelessly to ensure they had everything they needed. She instilled in me the importance of being kind and forgiving while also emphasizing the need to follow through on my commitments.

I remember spending a weekend at my Nan's house when I was about nine years old. We realized that I didn’t have anything suitable to wear to church the following day. So, on Sunday morning, Nan got up early and began sewing a skirt for me. I woke up to find her bent over her sewing machine, one of the older models with a foot pedal. Though the skirt wasn’t anything spectacular, as she used the materials she had on hand, what made it truly special was that every stitch reflected the love and dedication she poured into it. That sweet image has remained etched in my memory as vivid as it was all those years ago.

You could say, I drew inspiration for the character of Louise Taylor from my own home, particularly from the remarkable qualities of my Nan. While Louise possesses her own unique traits and special talents, both women share a strong will and unwavering faith. They are, indeed, women of noble character.

Many women do noble things,

but you surpass them all.

Charm is deceptive, and beauty is fleeting.

but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised.

posts.title: Torn Robes: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.
posts.content:
Who is Pastor John Wheaton, the main character in the Torn Robes book series?

In the prologue of the first book, "Torn Robes: Choices," we are introduced to Pastor John Wheaton. He is going through an incredibly challenging time. John is struggling with doubts about how to guide his congregation, fulfill his responsibilities as a father, and cope with feelings of anger toward God, to whom he has devoted his life to serve.

 I won't go into detail about the specifics— you'll have to read the book to find out. However, it's clear that his faith is being challenged in profound ways.

At 43 years old, John stands six feet tall. He has brown wavy hair, with some gray starting to appear at his temples. His hazel eyes and two prominent dimples add to his charm. John was born and raised in the fictional town of Homestead, Nebraska. I wanted John's character to showcase a mix of humor and fear, particularly his intense fear of flying! In addition, he needed to possess a deep sense of loyalty to his friends, family, and students. Above all, he had to embody the qualities of a man after God's own heart, much like David.

I drew inspiration for John’s character from a young parish priest who, for a time, had a profound influence in our town. This priest had a special gift for connecting with everyone, from teenagers to the entire community.  His personality was charismatic, compassionate, and engaging, and for a season, he shaped and impacted countless lives.

The same can be said of Pastor John Wheaton. When he travels to Gilford with his daughter Sarah to speak at a School Board meeting, he does so not only as a pastor but also as the best-selling author of "Children of The King."  This book draws on his interactions with the Homestead youth group and reflects the insights he gained as a father of a teenage girl. Below is a small excerpt from John's meeting with the Gilford School Board.

“Children crave attention, and they’ll get it by being good or bad.”  Shocked faces told John all he needed to know. “Look, you didn’t bring me all the way out here to sugar-coat your predicament. Did you?”

“No, we did not.” Came the lone voice from the other end of the table. John smiled at Louise. Nodding his head toward her in a manner of thanks for the support.

“Someone must tell them NO, but you also have to give them alternatives. Will it be easy? No. But will it be worth it? Yes!” John felt sympathy for their predicament. “Stop talking at them and start listening to them. Show them that their life is worth living. As adults, we need to listen more.”

Like a deflated balloon, John felt spent but before he left, he wanted the board members to know it wasn’t easy for him either. “Before our youth group was formed, I was oblivious to any problems, questions or feelings teenagers might have. And I’m the father of one of those teenagers. Haven’t we all been there?  We forget how much they look to us to answer their questions, no matter how silly they may sound or how hard the questions can be. If we aren’t there for these kids one way or another those questions will get answered. But they shouldn’t get answered from other teenagers guessing at the very same questions.”

John's wife, Abby, believed God had something special planned for his life. Initially, he thought his purpose was tied to the work he was doing in Homestead, but he soon realized that it was just the beginning. She had told him, "Never give up or back away from your path. Embrace it, and God will direct you." These words rang especially true considering what John was about to encounter in Gilford.

I’ll leave you with this short yet insightful scene between Tags, one of the students at Gilford High School, and Pastor John. This moment offers a profound insight into the character of the man, revealing his willingness to admit that he doesn't have all the answers…and that's okay.

Looking off into the emptiness of the room John stared into the pain still lurking within the walls of his heart. “No Tags, I don’t have everything. Believe it or not, I’m really just like you. Behind this infectious smile you see,” John joked, “I have questions and doubts, too.”

posts.title: Torn Robes - A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.
posts.content:
Every story needs a character that readers love to hate, and the Torn Robes book series is no different in that respect. Even though there are a few within the storylines, Linda Gallo is in a class all her own. She is a stay-at-home mother of teenage twins: Brittany, the captain of the cheerleading squad, and Joshua, the quarterback for the Gilford High School Bulldogs football team. Linda is the wife of a powerful Boston attorney, and her days are filled with hair, nail, and shopping appointments. She always wears the finest clothes and accessories that perfectly complement her outfits. Classic and very Vogue, Linda is constantly planning events and parties that become the talk of the town. The family spends a lot of time at the Gilford Country Club. However, Linda is also known throughout the town for her meddling and creating chaos. She is a hurricane-force wind, sweeping away anyone who stands in her way. I believe you understand the type of woman I’m referring to: the classic “don’t mess with me or I’ll destroy you” archetype.

In creating Linda's character, I modeled her look after my great-aunt Marie. She was always impeccably put together, embodying classic style and grace. However, that is where the similarities between their characters come to an end. My great-aunt was gracious, loving, and extremely generous. She passed away at the age of 102, just a month before her 103rd birthday. After her passing, I read her memoir and discovered that she and my grandmother, whom I adored, were best friends. I always thought they met while Aunt Marie's brother, my grandfather, was dating my grandmother. During their teenage summers, they spent time in Kennebunkport, Maine, working as "salad girls" and earning $50 a month, which came with room and board. The thought of her and my grandmother together, leaving behind the burdens of the Depression and enjoying their teenage years, warms my heart.

It's funny—my great aunt Marie wanted to be a journalist and travel the world. Instead, she became the wife of a Boston lawyer and the mother of three amazing children. Between the two of them, my great-aunt Marie was undoubtedly more glamorous than my grandmother. Nan was more of a tomboy; however, both of them possessed generous souls.

That is where the character comparison between my great aunt Marie and Linda Gallo ends. Although Linda Gallo feels it is her duty to help the less fortunate, her soul is cold in doing so.

Linda certainly meets her match when she encounters Pastor John. His character is the opposite of hers, which only frustrates her further. She is accustomed to people agreeing with her and not questioning her judgment or her family.

Below is the exchange between Linda Gallo and Pastor John during their first meeting in Torn Robes: Choices. After exchanging pleasantries, they delve into the reason for this meeting.

“John. Mrs. Gallo contacted Karl after her daughter Brittany came home from school the other day, very upset over something that had happened in your class. Do you recall anything out of the ordinary that would have upset Brittany?”

“Out of the ordinary?” came a sarcastic reply. “He humiliated my daughter in front of the entire class. Unless of course, that’s how he conducts his classes. Then I suppose it wouldn’t be out of the ordinary. Would it Pastor John?” She finished her sentence with such venom John was taken aback.

At the end of the scene, after John explains his side of the story, the following occurs:

Jumping to her feet Linda glared at John with contempt. “I don’t believe you and I don’t care about your opinion. It means nothing to me. You owe me an apology, and you owe my daughter Brittany an apology.” Unabashed, Linda waited for an apology.

John decided not to stand up knowing he would tower a good foot over Linda. He stayed seated and simply stated the facts.

“I cannot apologize for something I did not do or say. I believe if anyone is owed an apology it is the student Brittany embarrassed.”

Linda looked from Karl to Kristina with bulging eyes. Her face tightened and a tinge of red spread across her cheeks. “This is not over Pastor John, you obviously don’t know who I am. I will have you thrown out of this school, out of this town and sent back to wherever it is you came from. Karl might be onto something, religion, in a public school. I don’t think so.” With one last glare she left Karl’s office with him running after her like a lap dog.

While Linda experiences many twists and turns throughout the Torn Robes book series, she will always remain a hurricane-force wind, sweeping away anyone who stands in her path.
posts.excerpt: In the sixth post of my blog, Exploring the Series: Torn Robes - A Journey Through the Characters and the Town, we examine the character of Pastor John's arch-nemesis. Allow me to introduce you to the formidable Linda Gallo.

posts.title: Exploring the Torn Robes Series: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.
posts.content:
Joseph Taglio, affectionately called "Tags" by his close friends, is one of my favorite characters. What makes Tags particularly interesting is that he is the first character I created without basing him on someone I know personally. He made his debut at the age of 17 in "Torn Robes: Choices."

Tags grew up in an orphanage, having been placed there as a toddler. Unfortunately, he was labeled as “trouble” by his peers and the town's elite from a very young age, which denied him any chance to prove himself. During his teenage years, he learned how to survive on the streets.

Tags is a handsome young man of Italian descent, with long dark hair that he usually keeps tied back in a sleek ponytail. However, for his picture, I chose to capture him without the ponytail. He has strong facial features and hazel eyes. His typical attire includes a brown leather bomber jacket, jeans, and either flannel shirts or T-shirts, depending on the weather, paired with boots. Tags lives in an apartment building owned by the man who fostered him, along with several other boys from the orphanage, after they reached their teenage years. During this time, they helped run drugs for the town kingpin, Mr. Robeesy.

I envisioned Tags’ character to be tough and streetwise, yet also somewhat naive in a childlike way. He never allowed anyone into his life until he met Erica, which is reflected in the following excerpt from the first book, "Torn Robes: Choices."

He learned at a young age to harden his heart, until he met Erica. Sweet, innocent Erica Gray. She had an uncanny way of making him feel important and significant.

He and Erica are the parents of a precious little girl named Madison. They are the most important people in his life, and he is determined to protect and care for them at all costs. The following scene from "Torn Robes: Choices" illustrates the motivation behind his commitment as he watches Erica push Madison on a swing in the playground.

Tags could see the playground from where he stood in the small knoll above. Watching the carefree scene play out before him, “Aah,” his heart swelled with pride and much love.

Gilford was a small town. Ever since he could remember he’d been labeled the small-town hood. The troublemaker. Always being shuffled from one foster home to another.

“Why do you cause so much trouble?” At first, Tags didn’t understand. He didn’t mean to cause trouble, it sort of happened.

Tags was about five years old when he took the neighbor’s dog out on an adventure. How did he know the police had been called to look for him? Soaked and covered in mud after galloping through a small area of marshland, Tags and the dog arrived home hours later. Exhausted. Instead of a strong warm hug of reassurance, he was greeted with a severe tongue lashing. Then he was sent to his cold, dark room without any food or a change of dry clothes. Left alone to cry himself to sleep.

“Don’t worry Madison, that will never happen to you, I promise,” Tags said as he looked down at the little girl.

Developing the character of Joey "Tags" Taglio has been a true highlight of writing the Torn Robes series. He is a unique character with a rugged presence. Beneath his tough exterior lies a deep and caring soul, despite his indifference. His growth throughout the series has been a rewarding experience, and I take great pride in the journey we have undertaken together.  

The scene below is an excerpt from the second book in the series "Torn Robes: Reckoning." Pastor John is overseeing the Detention Room and is having a private conversation with Tags, a regular in the class.

John leaned over Tags’ desk and whispered for only his ears. “Tags, tell me you’re not doing anything illegal.”

“Pastor, I won’t say either way. A man’s got to do what a man’s gotta do. For his family. You understand?”

“No, I don’t understand. Does Erica understand?” John shook his head.

“You leave Erica out of this. I told you before I’m my own man.” Tags jumped up.

Grabbing Tags by his coat sleeve, John escorted him out of the classroom and into the hallway. Directing the class to open their books and get some homework done as he walked him out.

Standing toe to toe, John asked. “What happened to depending on God for everything?”

Tags stood there with no answer.

John continued. “What happened to giving him reign over your life?”

Still nothing.

There was a battle raging, the battle for his soul, and John recognized it. “Tags, remember… Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.” Philippians 4:6

“God’s timetable. My crossroad.” He left John to stare after him, walking toward the exit sign. Tags looked like a little boy, John thought.

I’ve shared examples of Tags from the first two books in the series, and now I’d like to leave you with a scene from the first chapter of the upcoming third book, "Torn Robes: The King's Highway." Tags plays a major role in this story. This scene takes place during the summer, as Erica and Tags take a walk with their daughter, Madison. Tags wants to ask Erica for her opinion on something he has been considering. Despite his tough exterior, Tags reveals to the reader that he has insecurities.

“Erica, if I tell you something you won’t laugh at me, will ya?”

“No, of course not. You sound serious, what is it?”

“I was thinking about when I was a little boy, moving from foster home to foster home and then back to the orphanage. I never had anyone to talk to. My only memories are of being yelled at, called names, or hit for one reason or another, and always feeling...alone.” He lifted his head to look at her. “What if I went there and asked if I could just hang out?” Tags couldn’t read her expression. He knew it was a dumb idea and regretted bringing it up.

After a moment Erica asked, “You mean like volunteering your time?”

“Yeah, isn’t that what I said?”

Tough and streetwise, yet somewhat naive in a childlike way.

I've included the song "Save My Life" by the Sidewalk Prophets, which plays an important role in book two, “Torn Robes: Reckoning”. I hope you take a moment to click on it and enjoy the music. I listened to it quite often while writing the second book.

Sidewalk Prophets - Save My Life (with lyrics)

posts.title: Exploring the Torn Robes Series: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town
posts.content:
Brittany is the daughter of Anthony and Linda Gallo. Linda, the antagonist in my first book, Torn Robes: Choices, was introduced in my sixth post.  She is also the twin sister of Joshua Gallo. You know the old saying, "the apple doesn't fall far from the tree," and in Brittany's case, that saying holds true. However, it is not her mother, Linda Gallo, that Brittany resembles most; rather, she is most like her father, Anthony Gallo.

Brittany is a complex character inspired by traits I’ve observed in past acquaintances and reflections of my own teenage experiences. She can be self-centered, superficial, and prideful. As the captain of the football cheerleading squad, Brittany is aware of her beauty and enjoys the admiration of her classmates. She has a sharp tongue and often taunts her peers, giving herself a false sense of power. This behavior ultimately leads to her personal revelations in the first book, "Torn Robes: Choices." Let’s take a look at how we first meet Brittany.

“Perfect,” Brittany Gallo announced. Her pleased reflection stared back at her from the long mirror mounted on the bedroom wall. As captain of the football cheerleading squad, she had a certain status and responsibility. “After all,” she reminded herself, “Daddy always says.” Brittany deepened her tone and pointed her right index finger at her reflection.

“’People look up to leaders, Brittany. You have an obligation to the less fortunate people of the world.’ Ah, so much responsibility.” With one last look in the mirror, Brittany sashayed out of her bedroom.

Her character experiences significant changes and growth throughout the Torn Robes series. Her first real challenge occurs during her initial class with Pastor John, who refuses to be intimidated by her bold intolerance. In Brittany's mind, he delivers the ultimate insult by calling her out in front of her classmates.

Brittany Gallo is a fascinating character whose complexities are revealed throughout the first two books in the Torn Robes series. In the third book, Torn Robes: The King's Highway, she plays a significant role. The earlier books, Torn Robes: Choices and Torn Robes: Reckoning, add depth to her character, making her journey a powerful force to be reckoned with.

I encourage you to take the opportunity to get to know Brittany Gallo; you won't be disappointed!

posts.title: "Exploring the Series: Torn Robes - A Journey Through the Characters and the Town."
posts.content:
I’m not entirely sure where Titus' character originated, but he came to life on the very first day of his junior year after summer break. The following excerpt is from "Torn Robes: Choices." In the auditorium filled with noisy students, an interaction unfolds between Titus and Pastor John. Just moments before, Pastor John was introduced as their new teacher for the elective class titled "God and Faith." This is where Titus' intellect and awkwardness began to take shape.

“Is this class supposed to force us to believe there is a God? Because I’d take the class just to show you there isn’t.” A young man with light brown skin and dark, dancing eyes, jumped up from his seat in the third row. The auditorium erupted with loud applause and offensive remarks.

Smiling at the young man, John replied in a loud voice to be heard over the lingering banter.

“What’s your name young man?”

“Titus Poor,” came the proud reply, feeling the crowd behind him.

“Titus, I take challenges very seriously and if you stand behind your words and mean what you say you’ve just become the first student to sign up for my class. I look forward to our time together. And to be clear Titus. You or any student thinking about taking this class will find out that I won’t be convincing anyone there’s a God. That’s a mountain too high for me to climb. I’ll leave that undertaking to God Himself.”

Titus adjusted his round-rimmed glasses not sure what just happened. He acknowledged John’s proposal with a slight nod and sat down after getting hi-fives and pats on his back.

As you can see, Titus's intelligence can sometimes be a double-edged sword. He is a determined young man, and nothing and no one will stand in the way of his ambition to become a leading scientist for NASA. Throughout the first and second books, Titus undergoes significant personal growth. We discover that he has a humorous and charming side. As he navigates some difficult decisions, he finds support in an unlikely new friend, Sharise Taylor. She helps him realize that there is more to life than just academics. In the following excerpt, Titus is taking a walk with Sharise, sharing without pause details about his heritage and the pride he feels in it.

“Are you serious Titus? You’re not pulling my leg?”

“No really. I don’t tell too many people. But yes, I’m a descendant of the great Salem Poor, a soldier of the Revolutionary War. He was a hero at the Battle of Bunker Hill. He literally had fourteen officers write to the Massachusetts legislature commending him for his bravery and gallantry. My family originally comes from Lexington and a lot of them still live there, but my mom and dad wanted to be near the water. So here I am.,” Titus exclaimed with an arm-sweeping bow.

Giggling at his display of chivalry, Sharise told him how impressed she was, understanding where the strength of his confidence and pride came from.

I have had a wonderful time developing Titus's character—something I did not expect. He is an observant individual who has no qualms about stating facts as he sees them. Titus loves to debate and firmly believes he is always right. However, during his journey, he discovers the true significance of friendship.

I would like to conclude this post in true Titus fashion, he would confidently proclaim, "Thank you for visiting! If you return for graduation, I guarantee you won’t want to miss my remarkable valedictorian speech." Although, Sharise might have a different opinion on that.

posts.title: Exploring the Series: Torn Robes - A Journey Through the Characters and the Town. Meet Sara Wheaton!
posts.content:
Sarah Wheaton is the kind of person who never speaks ill of anyone. She is a devoted and loyal friend, as well as a loving daughter to Pastor John Wheaton, embodying kindness, and compassion. Beneath her cheerful exterior, however, lies a deep emotional wound that will never fully heal. Through time and prayer, she has learned to navigate her pain. Her friends and family are always her priority, and she chooses to keep her struggles hidden from them.

As Sarah's character developed, I imagined her with the face of an angel. Hence the picture shows her looking out at the ocean, because I could not find a face that matched what was in my head. I envisioned her with flowing blond hair, deep blue eyes and two prominent dimples like her father's. More importantly, a heart as fierce and determined as Esther's. Born and raised in the fictional town of Homestead, Nebraska, Sarah adds a unique depth to the Torn Robes Series.

During her stay in Gilford, she becomes best friends with Sharise Taylor. Their friendship is strong and enduring, much like the bond between sisters. Sarah also attracts the attention of Gilford High's quarterback, Joshua Gallo, who happens to be the twin brother of Brittany Gallo, a character we met earlier. The following excerpt describes the moment Sarah and Joshua first see each other. Both Sharise and Sarah are helping Pastor John set up the classroom for his course, "God and Faith." Sarah went downstairs to meet Sharise and bring her up to the classroom on the third floor.

Sarah and Sharise sprinted toward the school doors. Sharise held the door open for Sarah who grabbed the door quickly, but not without taking a quick glance behind her. For the briefest of moments, she locked eyes with one of the football players exiting the gym.

“Come on, before they realize we’ve left the area.” Sharise urged pulling Sarah from her powerful encounter.

Sarah is strong and very protective of her father. She loves telling stories, especially Bible stories, is musically inclined like her mother and adds logic to conversations with wisdom beyond her years. The following excerpt illustrates the strong character of Sarah Wheaton. It takes place in the living room of the Taylor home and captures a pivotal moment as Pastor John grapples with a crisis at school. His students want to show their support and let the people of Gilford know how much he means to them. However, Pastor John is reluctant to involve the students. In this critical moment, Sarah speaks up.

“I believe we were all meant to be right here.” Sarah said, looking around the room. “Right now. At this exact moment. Dad, your students are going to be at that meeting tomorrow night. Nothing is going to stop them. Their words and actions will define their character in front of parents, teachers, and peers. Will they leave the meeting trusting in God or harden themselves against Him?”

John got up from his seat then went to his daughter and wrapped his arms around her. He said in a choked whisper, “Your mother would be very proud of the wise young lady you’ve become. As proud as I am at this very moment.”

Dwayne passed the tissues to Tracie and Louise, even Scott was affected.

Always listen to Sarah, Sharise made a mental note.

Sarah's character embodies the ideal person I wish I had been in my youth and beyond. She is also a blend of the qualities I admire in my dear friends and family members. Sarah represents the conscience that guides us all and the standard we strive to achieve.

So, what does happen between Sarah Wheaton and Joshua Gallo? Does he pursue her? How does Pastor John react to their growing connection? More importantly, what are Sarah's true feelings about the highly popular football star? Here is a teaser to spark your curiosity.

“I had a great time tonight. I’m glad I came.” Joshua finally got to speak with Sarah alone.

“It was a lot of fun, I’m glad you came, too,” Sarah said returning his smile. Thinking it was a long shot Sarah asked anyway. “Would you like to come to service Sunday? There’s great music. Everyone is so nice, and my dad will be preaching.”

“I don’t know Sarah, church has never been my thing.” Adding quickly not wanting to miss his chance, “But maybe we could go out for a movie sometime?”

Sarah’s heart did a deep dive into the pit of her stomach. Her face glowed a slight tinge of red, she should’ve known better. Sarah swallowed hard. “Joshua, I like you. I do. But church is my thing, you can’t have one without the other. So, friends?” Sarah extended her hand in friendship, but that was as far as the relationship could go.

Joshua’s eyes traveled from Sarah’s extended hand into her beguiling blue eyes. Ignoring her hand, he asked, “What time on Sunday?”
posts.excerpt:
Angels appear in many different forms to hold your hand through the difficult times. Doreen Virtue
Meet Sarah Wheaton!

posts.title: Exploring the Series: Torn Robes - A Journey Through the Characters and the Town
posts.content:
Joshua Gallo is Brittany Gallo's twin brother and the son of Anthony and Linda Gallo. He is the type of boy that many girls have a crush on, though he remains unaware of it. Tall and attractive, he has cropped black hair, golden-brown eyes, and a captivating smile. Oh, and he happens to be the quarterback of the Gilford High School football team. I didn’t know how Joshua’s character would develop—would he be a nice guy, a bad guy, or just indifferent? The storyline seemed to unfold on its own.

Joshua is humorous and, like any growing boy, loves to eat. He is selective about who he dates, not because he is arrogant, but rather because he is waiting for the right girl. The following excerpt is from Torn Robes: Choices, where he realizes that the girl who caught his eye is Pastor John’s daughter.

Joshua sat in the back of the auditorium like always. His attention went to center stage when Pastor John stepped up to the microphone to answer Kimberly’s question. Joshua followed the pastor’s eyes when he was finished speaking. They rested on the girl he saw walking into the school a few days earlier with Sharise. “Father and daughter, got it.” He whispered.

Joshua loves football and is part of a talented team, which includes his best friend, wide receiver extraordinaire, Steve Marchetti. However, that’s a story for another time. Joshua is confused about the unexpected feelings he has for Sarah, a girl he has never met. He is determined to connect with her and goes to great lengths to achieve this, even rearranging his schedule to be in the same class as Sarah, as illustrated in the following excerpt from “Choices.”

“Look Joshua, I understand you want to take this class, but you’ll have to change your whole schedule around to fit it in. There are other electives you can choose that go perfectly with your schedule,” Amy Curtis pleaded.

“I understand. Although I would hate to miss the opportunity to learn from someone the School Board thought was so good that they asked him to move all the way from Nebraska to teach at Gilford High.”

“Okay. Okay. Far be it from me to hold a student back from greatness.”

“Thank you.”

Amy started the process, turning his schedule upside down.

Joshua has aa remarkable ability to persuade others, especially when the situation demands it. Just look at how he convinces his best friend to join him in taking Pastor John’s class instead of opting for a study hall. I mean, what student would choose an elective class over a study period?

What class do you have Tuesdays and Thursday mornings at 10:05?" Joshua asked.

“I don’t know. I haven’t even looked.” Steve pulled out a crumbled piece of paper from his pocket. Joshua grabbed the paper out of his hand.

“Let me see?”

“Hey dude, what are you doing?

“Look. You have study hall. You should take that God and Faith class with me.”

Steve laughed. “Are you nuts? Why would I want to do that instead of having a free period?”

“Because you would get two easy credits for doing nothing. Then you could get that free period next year when we’re seniors.” Joshua could see the wheels turning in Steve's head knowing he wanted to coast through his senior year.

“You think it’s an easy class?”

“Come on, how hard can it be? We went to Sunday school. We already know the answers. Look, I’ll even go to the office with you to get it changed.”

Without skipping a beat, Joshua put his arm around Steve’s shoulders and led him toward the school office.

Very persuasive indeed!

Most high schools offer an array of sports for students to participate in, and Gilford High is no exception, particularly since the fictional town of Gilford is situated in Massachusetts. Without hesitation, I can honestly say New Englanders are incredibly serious about their sports teams. The Red Sox took 85 years to win their first World Series, the Bruins have claimed six Stanley Cups, and the Celtics have won 18 NBA Championships. I love them all, but if I had to choose one sport, it would definitely be football. I am passionate about the game, regardless of whether it's at the college or professional level.

In the days following September 11, 2001, our nation was in a state of shock and disbelief. We were a wounded nation, but through our pain, we remained steadfast and united. Initially, the NFL was uncertain about the football season that year. However, it did take place, and after forty long years, the New England Patriots won their first Super Bowl. It was a miraculous playoff run and Super Bowl victory for a team named the Patriots. The word 'Patriot' embodies the spirit of our Founding Fathers and reflects the resilience of a nation that was brought to its knees just months earlier by a terrorist attack. Although one football game did not heal our nation, that victory lifted our spirits. Sometimes, God shines a light in the darkness, reminding us of who we are and what we fight for: God, family, and country.

Throughout the Torn Robes Series, Joshua grows as a friend, a brother, and a son. During a class discussion, Pastor John changes direction from his original question to explain the meaning of the name “Joshua.” He takes this opportunity to help Joshua see the significance of his name.

“Do you know what the name Joshua means?” John side-stepped for a moment.

“No,” Joshua answered shrugging his shoulders. He shared, “I never thought about it before.”

“In the Bible, Joshua is depicted as a great leader, a deliverer of his people. Joshua was appointed by God because he was a strong leader and a patient man. Patience is a great character to have in a leader on and off the field.”

“I’ll try and remember that.”

“Don’t just remember it, Joshua. Live it.”

Does Joshua rise to the challenge of becoming a true leader? I can't reveal that—no spoiler alerts here! See you in the next post!

posts.title: Exploring the Series: Torn Robes - A Journey Through the Characters and the Town
posts.content:
Sharise Taylor

Throughout her youth, Sharise had many friends but, everything changed when Pastor John and Sarah came from Nebraska to stay at the Taylor home. In Sarah Wheaton, Sharise found a sister. They truly complement each other—like yin and yang. Until her junior year, her father and older brother, D.J., were the main male figures in her life. That is, until she encountered Titus on the first day of Pastor John's class, "God and Faith." The following is an excerpt from the first book in the Torn Robes Series, "Choices.” Pastor John is recounting the events on the Day of Pentecost to the class and Titus is having none of it:

“The apostles had no reason to lie about something this extraordinary. Explain to me Titus, how could eleven frightened and confused men leave that upper room after receiving the Holy Spirit? No matter how we envision it happened, they received a passion and urgency to share the good news of Jesus. They were no longer confused or afraid. They had determination and an eagerness, to share their newfound love, knowledge and understanding.”

“I don’t know. Maybe they thought they would become famous or something, like Jesus,” Titus volunteered.

“Famous?” Sharise quipped. “Yeah, famous enough to get killed.”

Sarah reached over and touched Sharise’s arm in an effort to calm her down.

“Famous? Sorry, Pastor John. Foolish is more like it!”

“It’s okay, Sharise. We have to give Titus and any other student in class the right to voice their opinions. No matter how much we agree or disagree. That’s how we’re going to learn from each other.”

Sharise gave John a nod of understanding, but John got the impression she didn’t like it.

I admire her spirit and the way she respectfully stands her ground.

As we progress further into the "Choices" story, we discover that Sharise has a deep passion for music, evident in everything from her ringtones to the playlists she creates. She is quite talented, and her love for Jesus is clearly expressed through the songs she sings. Sharise is not shy about expressing her faith through her music and knows how to make an entrance. It's Fall Festival at Cornerstone Church and Sharise has entered the building.

Suddenly the church was alive with the sound of music.

Shaking her head, Sarah knew it could only mean one thing, Sharise had arrived. Flinging open the office door, John greeted the merry group parading through the entry way. Sharise and Shelby led the group singing with unabashed passion, fists pumping high in the air.

Brittany politely tapped Sharise on the shoulder, who was oblivious to everything around her except the song in her heart.

Believe it or not, Sharise can be patient when the other person is willing to listen. In "Choices," Sharise and Titus take a walk to the quarry and find themselves discussing Titus's faith, or lack thereof. In this excerpt, Titus tries to explain to Sharise why he doesn't believe in God.

Sharise hesitantly asked, “Titus? I know you don’t believe in God, but can I ask why?”

He stopped before moving through a doorway of stone that led to a view of rocky cliffs, a grand display of rushing water and white foam sprayed up over the jagged edges. Titus bent down and picked something up.

“Sharise,” He opened his hand displaying the grayish blue stone, dusty and dirty from the trail. “I like to know when I pick up a rock it’s a rock, not because someone tells me it’s a rock but because I can feel it and see it’s a rock. Believing in someone or something because someone tells me it’s true doesn’t make it true. Especially up here.” He said pointing to the top of his head. “I need evidence. Proof.”

Sharise reflects on what Titus has just said as they walk together along the outer edge of the quarry before she responds.

“Remember the story Pastor told us the very first day in class about Thomas one of Jesus’ disciples.”

Titus shrugged his shoulders.

“He wasn’t with the other apostles when Jesus first appeared to them and when the other apostles told Thomas what had happened, he replied ‘Until I put my hands through the holes of his hands and feet or the hole where he was pierced on his side, I will not believe.’”

“That I can understand.”

“Well, the next time Jesus appeared to the apostles, Thomas was there. Jesus went up to Thomas held out his hands showing the holes where the nails went through and he urged Thomas to touch them and to touch where the spear pierced his side. Trembling, Thomas dropped to his knees crying, ‘Forgive me Lord I believe. I believe.’ Jesus said to Thomas, ‘You believe because you see.’” Sharise said opening her hand to reveal the rock she was holding. "Blessed are those who believe and do not see.” Sharise dropped the rock. Titus was about to reply when Sharise put her finger to his mouth and said, “Shh. Let it be.”

I intentionally remained in “Torn Robes: Choices” because I believed it was important to share Sharise's beginnings and the development of the relationships that are important to her journey. In the upcoming third book of the series, “The King's Highway,” Sharise is about to enter her senior year of high school. Like many teenagers, she has doubts about herself, her future, and the dreams she has yet to reveal.

Sharise's character is drawn from the memories and reflections of my youth, influenced by my friends who, like me, were surrounded by music, friendship, competition, and sisterly bonds. Those experiences and cherished moments deeply shaped my perspective and discipline; without them, I know I would be a completely different person today.

posts.title: The Blog Rests While We Prepare Our Hearts
posts.content:
As Advent unfolds, I’m choosing to pause my blog series, Torn Robes: A Journey Through the Characters and the Town.”

Just as stories rest between chapters, so too will this blog rest, allowing space for reflection, prayer, and the gentle hush of anticipation. The characters and town of Torn Robes will also wait with us, carrying their hopes and longings into the silence.

I’ll return after New Year's with new insights, stories, and fresh glimpses into their lives—but not before leaving you with a gift. Below is an exchange between Pastor John Wheaton and his students from the second book in the series, Reckoning, as they begin their Christmas break. His words, I believe, capture the very heart of this season.

“The Christmas season, although you may not know it, is not all presents and decorations,” John said, much to his student’s chagrin.

“What?”

“Come on!”

“I know, I know, what was I thinking? Seriously, it should also be a time of reflection and prayer. It’s a time to listen for God’s voice and direction. And that is what I intend to do.”

May peace, joy, and the light of His promise fill your days.

K.M. Quinn
posts.excerpt: Honoring Advent with quiet anticipation and hope.
